title_header: The Blood of Ae Butterfly - Episode 2: Parasite

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Control again?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Yeah, I think there was an accident at school. The kids that Maehwa beat up are still unconscious.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN (Adult)
dialogue.line: But those kids must have seen Maehwa use his power. We have to do something before The Garden finds out about this. Please, I need your help.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN (Adult)
dialogue.line: Here's the stuff you asked for. There's enough for three people.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Thank you. Are you sure this can manipulate their memories?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN (Adult)
dialogue.line: Yeah, although it only works on their short-term memory.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN (Adult)
dialogue.line: Huina, don't tell anyone about this. But if something like this happens again and someone finds out, I won't be able to help you. There's only so much I can do for you.

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: Okay. I'll keep that in mind.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: FIVE YEARS LATER

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: BIG BUILDING CONVENIENCE STORE

dialogue.speaker: JINGIE
dialogue.line: Can you believe that?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Hahaha.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Welcome.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: What should I get?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Why bother eating? All the food is just nasty crap made by The Garden anyway.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Man, I heard that convenience stores used to have so much good food 20 years ago. Why did we have to be born now? It sucks. My poor taste buds! It's all The Big's fault!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Oh right, someone got attacked by a bug around here just a couple of days ago. And apparently it was one of those parasites that looks like a person.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Seriously?! That's so creepy!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: But how can you tell if someone's a parasite? They steal people's bodies and look just like them.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Well, it's hard to tell for sure. But they act kind of differently from other people.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: That's freaky.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: We're ready to pay.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Hold on. You've been acting kind of funny today. You're not as dumb as usual.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Screw you!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Hahaha.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: That'll be 3,500 won, about 3 USD.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Wait a minute, aren't you Maehwa Baek?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: It's you, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you're right! I can't believe this.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Huh? You know this guy?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Yeah, back in middle school, he got a little cut, so he practically killed some guys in his class.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Looks like you're doing all right for yourself even after getting expelled.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Hey, I'm curious. Do you still go on killing sprees when you see blood? Huh?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Hey, wait.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Maybe you're... a bug. There are lots of bugs that pretend to be human! What are they called again? Parasites? They act normal, but they go around killing people! That sounds a lot like you! Am I right?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Killed your friends! My...

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Hey, take it easy. He might try and hurt us too.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: You son of a bitch!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: I don't want to eat anything you've touched!

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: You goddamn bug.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Man, I'm so pissed. Let's go somewhere else.

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: So they think I'm a bug, huh? 'Do you still go on killing sprees when you see blood? Huh?' 'I don't want to eat anything you've touched.' 'You goddamn bug.'

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: My blood. I've spent my whole life worrying about blood. Afraid I might accidentally cut myself. I've always carried a tablet in my bag instead of paper books and writing tools. I've avoided any sports with high risk of injury, and anything that involves using sharp tools. Especially since that incident in middle school. I can't ever see my own blood. I have to constantly be on my guard, even if it stresses me out every single day. But in spite of all that, people still treat me like I'm a bug. But it's okay because I have Huina. Which reminds me, what should I do with this drawing? Huina will be upset if she sees it.

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: Oh, you're home, Maehwa! Come on in.

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: Hey, Huina.

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: You surprised me! You're home early. I didn't think you'd be here. Are you going somewhere?

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: Oh, I bought a cake, but I forgot to get something to drink.

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: A cake?

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: Tada! Check it out! Doesn't it look good?

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: Y-yeah, but cakes are so hard to come by nowadays. Where did you get it from?

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: I had to go somewhere really far away. Today's your birthday, remember?

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: Huh? Is it?

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: Don't tell me you forgot your own birthday.

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: Hmm? Oh, you brought your tablet with you? Were you drawing? You haven't drawn anything in a while.

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: Oh, I--

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: Let me see.

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: I-I just... drew something on my break.

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: Uh oh. I'm in trouble now. This will be just like last time.

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: Maehwa, this is... awesome! Beautiful! I love the red! Although I don't have much of an eye for art.

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: Huh? What?

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: I'm just going to get us something to drink. Have a seat!

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: Wait!

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: What is it?

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: I'll get the drinks. You can stay here.

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: Huh? Why?

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: This area is dangerous. I heard some sketchy rumors earlier.

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: Sketchy rumors? What did you hear?

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: For some reason, in that moment, I suddenly remembered what they were saying earlier: 'They act kind of differently from normal people.'

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: Hey, Huina.

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: I thought you hated the color red.

dialogue.speaker: HUINA
dialogue.line: What? What do you mea--

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: You've always said that ever since I was little. Red reminds you of blood. It makes you nauseous. It reminds you of what I did before. You were the one who wanted to get rid of everything that reminded you of that. Something's not right. Tell me right now: Who are you?

dialogue.speaker: PARASITE (as Huina)
dialogue.line: Aw, too bad. Smeh. I got caught.

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: Ah. I messed up.

dialogue.speaker: PARASITE (as Huina)
dialogue.line: But it doesn't matter! I've already completely taken over this human! I worked so hard to get this body.

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: It's a parasite!

dialogue.speaker: PARASITE (as Huina)
dialogue.line: I'm going to eat lots of little weaklings like you, so that I can grow big and strong!

dialogue.speaker: PARASITE (as Huina)
dialogue.line: Aw, poor little guy.

dialogue.speaker: PARASITE (as Huina)
dialogue.line: If you knew I was a bug, you should have pretended not to know and run away.

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: How the hell did this happen?! When did it get Huina?! How on Earth?! Why Huina of all people?! Wait, no, that's not important right now! All that matters now is getting as far away as possible!

dialogue.speaker: PARASITE (as Huina)
dialogue.line: Got you.

dialogue.speaker: PARASITE (as Huina)
dialogue.line: You naughty little boy.

dialogue.speaker: PARASITE (as Huina)
dialogue.line: I've told you a thousand times. Don't walk away when people are talking to you.

dialogue.speaker: PARASITE (as Huina)
dialogue.line: Say your prayers.

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: I can't breathe!

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: People?

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: It seems kind of funny to hear those words come out of a bug's mouth, don't you think?

dialogue.speaker: PARASITE (as Huina)
dialogue.line: You--

dialogue.speaker: MAEHWABAEK
dialogue.line: You mean, don't walk away when bugs are talking. Not people. But no one cares what bugs have to say. Sorry to break it to you, you filthy bug.

summary: Maehwa Baek, haunted by a past incident involving blood, is confronted by former classmates who accuse him of being a 'bug' or parasite. Later, while celebrating his birthday with his friend Huina, Maehwa realizes that Huina has been replaced by a parasite, leading to a violent confrontation.
